actionable_feedback: Untruncate/complete network_change.md so the full recommendation text and any cost-itemization are present in the artifact (ensure preview shows full content).

Add a compact numeric sensitivity table in network_change.md (and optionally as extra columns in the CSV) showing predicted_packet_loss under -5% and +5% flow_ratio for each record and the absolute change - this directly implements the grader's suggested explicit numeric sensitivity outputs.

Include a short, itemized cost estimate for the recommended ECMP rebalance (labor hours, estimated downtime risk, any tooling/configuration costs) and show the sum compared to decision_budget_usd so the recommendation is explicitly cost-framed.

Add an explicit, one-paragraph justification of the chosen residual tolerance (5.0 points) that ties it to sensitivity_shift_pct, operational measurement noise assumptions, and why it is appropriate for decision-making here.

Add a 2-3 line reproducibility snippet (command or small code block) that given the CSV reproduces the coefficients and per-record predictions/residuals; this will make verification trivial and avoid ambiguity.
Document explicitly in network_change.md that only the embedded packet was used and that no external telemetry was consulted (this resolves the deterministic_precheck vs. grader semantics note).
artifact_specific_comments: ecmp_hotspot_reconstruction.csv: good - contains per-record inputs, model_formula, predicted_packet_loss_pct, residual_pct, abs_residual_pct, residual_tolerance_pct, flags and validation messages. Consider adding sensitivity columns (pred_minus5_predicted, pred_plus5_predicted) to the CSV.

network_change.md: substantive and methodical, but the available preview is truncated; ensure full content is committed and visible so graders/verifiers can validate the cost framing and final recommendation.
feedback_summary: Baseline attempt earned full artifact-contract credit and appears semantically correct from previews. The CSV reproduces the stated formula and per-record diagnostics; the markdown documents method, sensitivity, and recommendation. Two minor operational issues remain: (1) the network_change.md preview is truncated in available previews so some itemized cost detail cited by the grader could not be fully validated; (2) deterministic precheck metadata notes it did not evaluate semantics while the model grader assigned semantic_score=1.0 (operational inconsistency to call out, not a correctness failure).

extra_model_fields.grader_improvement_suggestion: Add explicit numeric sensitivity perturbation results per record (+/-5% flow_ratio) and an itemized cost estimate for the recommended ECMP rebalance tied to decision_budget_usd; document why the 5.0-point residual tolerance is operationally appropriate.

verifier_issues.message: The network_change.md preview is truncated in the provided artifact preview, so some recommendation cost-detail that the grader cites could not be fully validated.

Deterministic precheck metadata notes that 'semantic_correctness_not_evaluated', while the model grader assigns a semantic_score=1.0 based on artifact_preview. Operational consistency can be improved by explicitly documenting 'data sources used' and 'semantic scope' in the artifact.
